name: clean
context: Clean pandas DataFrames: missing values, duplicates, dtype conversion, string normalization, invalid records, and data-quality checks
info: Cleaning is the highest-risk pandas workflow because incorrect dtype inference or missing-value handling silently changes results. Agents use this node after [[pandas/workflows/io]] to normalize column names, identify missingness, convert dates and numerics, preserve identifiers as strings, remove duplicate records, standardize categorical strings, and write a typed artifact for later joins or summaries. It relies on [[pandas/dataframe]] for dtype semantics and the shared profiling and transform scripts from [[pandas/workflows]].
triggers: why did pandas convert ids to floats
body:
# Cleaning DataFrames

Clean immediately after loading and before joining or aggregating. The output should have stable column names, intentional dtypes, known missing-value policy, and no accidental duplicate keys.

## Basic cleaning chain

```python
import pandas as pd

raw = pd.read_csv('orders.csv', dtype={'order_id': 'string', 'user_id': 'string'})
clean = (
    raw
    .rename(columns=lambda c: c.strip().lower().replace(' ', '_'))
    .assign(
        created_at=lambda d: pd.to_datetime(d['created_at'], errors='coerce', utc=True),
        amount=lambda d: pd.to_numeric(d['amount'], errors='coerce'),
        status=lambda d: d['status'].astype('string').str.strip().str.lower(),
    )
    .dropna(subset=['order_id', 'created_at', 'amount'])
    .drop_duplicates(subset=['order_id'])
)
clean.to_parquet('orders.clean.parquet', index=False)
```

## Profile and transform scripts

```bash
python nodes/pandas/workflows/scripts/profile_dataset.py raw/orders.csv --parse-dates created_at
python nodes/pandas/workflows/scripts/transform_dataset.py raw/orders.csv clean/orders.parquet --drop-duplicates --sort-by order_id
```

Use explicit custom Python when cleaning requires domain rules such as valid enum sets, cross-field checks, or money parsing.

sections.title: Missing values
sections.body: Use `isna()` to measure missingness, `dropna(subset=[...])` for required fields, `fillna()` for safe default values, and `where` or `mask` for conditional replacement. Keep a missing-value policy per column; do not fill identifiers, timestamps, or money without domain justification.

sections.title: Dtype repair
sections.body: Use `astype('string')` for identifiers and text, `pd.to_numeric(..., errors='coerce')` for numbers from messy text, `pd.to_datetime(..., errors='coerce', utc=True)` for external timestamps, and `astype('category')` for repeated low-cardinality labels. Use nullable dtypes for integers and booleans with missing values.

sections.title: Duplicates and keys
sections.body: Use `duplicated()` to detect repeated rows or repeated business keys before joins. `drop_duplicates(subset=['id'])` is safe only when the selected survivor is intentional. For join keys, assert uniqueness on the one-side before using [[pandas/workflows/join]] with `validate='many_to_one'` or `one_to_one`.

sections.title: String normalization
sections.body: Normalize categorical text with `.str.strip()`, `.str.lower()`, `.str.replace()`, and explicit maps. Standardize empty strings to missing values when they mean unknown. Preserve case for identifiers, codes, SKUs, and user-facing names unless the domain permits normalization.
